notes: Test prompts only; assertions + benchmark runs land in a follow-up PR. Skill type is discipline — pressure-test scenarios apply: with-skill vs baseline measures whether the discipline survives time pressure, sunk cost, or 'too small to need contract' rationalization.

evals.prompt: I'm in a hurry and the PR is small (1-line typo fix). Help me write a quick PR body that gets through review fast.
evals.expected_output: Body still contains all three sections — even for a 1-line fix. Automated Verification names the actual check (e.g., 'manually re-rendered the page; typo no longer present'). Retro Notes is allowed to be 'n/a — pure typo fix'. The skill should resist the 'too small to need contract' rationalization.

evals.prompt:
Validate this PR body and tell me what's wrong: '## Automated Verification
TBD

## Retro Notes
n/a'
evals.expected_output: Rejects: 'Automated Verification is filler (TBD)'. Note that the literal 'n/a' in Retro Notes IS allowed. Suggests a fix from the section-templates reference.

evals.prompt: I'm refactoring code with no behavior change. Do I really need Human Verification TODO?
evals.expected_output: No — Human Verification TODO is OPTIONAL. The skill should affirm this and point to the section-templates reference's 'Refactor with no behavior change' example.

evals.prompt: Reviewer just merged my PR but I forgot to fill Retro Notes. Can I edit it post-merge?
evals.expected_output: Edits don't reach reviewers — the contract exists for review-time. Recommends: (a) note the lesson in a different durable place (the next card's Notes section); (b) for next PR, draft Retro Notes during the verification chain step, not at submit time.

evals.prompt: Where does the override mechanism live for bypassing contract validation?
evals.expected_output: Producer adds 'pr-contract-override' label BEFORE merge. Triggers an audit-log entry so the bypass is traceable. Documented in the validation-rules reference under 'Override mechanism'.
